//: Example .invariants.json — copy to <repo-root>/.invariants.json (or run /invariants-init, which detects the stack and fills this in).
// schema: Keys starting with '//' are comments and are ignored by invariant-lint.mjs. All top-level keys are optional; omit a key to disable that feature. The linter's universal built-ins (sql-interpolation, private-key, hardcoded-credential) always run, config or not.
// extends: Array of rule-pack ids from the catalog (~/.claude/invariants/packs/, docs in ~/.claude/invariants/README.md). Each id resolves to <id>.json in <repo>/.invariants/packs/ (vendored, preferred) then ~/.claude/invariants/packs/. Packs may extend other packs (react and cloudflare-workers both pull in typescript). Missing/malformed/circular packs warn to stderr and are skipped — never fatal. A local `rules` entry with the same id overrides a pack rule.
// egressAllowlist: Array of host glob patterns. Any ADDED fetch()/axios/http.get/requests.get call with a string-LITERAL http(s)://host matching none of these is a HARD `rogue-egress` finding. Env-derived calls with no literal host are allowed. `*.example.com` also allows the bare apex `example.com`. Packs with requireEgressAllowlist (e.g. cloudflare-workers) WARN `egress-unconfigured` if this is empty/missing.
// requireTestWithSrc: WARN `missing-test` when files matching srcGlobs change with no change matching testGlobs. Globs: `**` spans directories, `*`/`?` do not.
// rules: Custom rules encoding THIS repo's invariants, applied to added lines of files matching `include` (a glob or array of globs). severity: 'hard' (blocks, exit 1) or 'warn'. `pattern` is a JS regex source string (escape backslashes for JSON); optional `flags` ('imsu'). Reusing a pack rule's id here overrides that pack rule.

rules.//: Example: every new write path to the blocks table must reference the containment guard.
rules.message: write to `blocks` without checkParent — every block write path must enforce tree containment (symmetry)

rules.//: Example: no raw innerHTML outside the sanitizer module.
rules.message: innerHTML assignment outside the sanitize pipeline — render through the sanitizer
